zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Matilda Urquhart of Troup, Baroness of Dunottar於1360年出生在蘇格蘭班福爾時，她的父親William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty35歲，母親Agnes Ramsey35歲。她於1379年與Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland在英國蘇格蘭奧可尼聖 安德魯斯結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1413年6月2日在英國蘇格蘭金加迪爾享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Matilda Urquhart of Troup, Baroness of Dunottar于1360年出生在苏格兰班福爾时，她的父亲William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty35岁，母亲Agnes Ramsey35岁。她于1379年与Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland在英国苏格兰奧可尼聖 安德魯斯结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1413年6月2日在英国苏格兰金加迪爾享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Urquhart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Ross and Cromarty): habitational name from any of various places in Scotland so called, notably the barony of Urquhart (Inverness), or places in Ross and Cromarty, Morayshire, and Fife. All are thought to derive from Pictish ar ‘on, by’ + Pictish carden ‘enclosure’ or ‘wood, copse’. Some bearers of this name trace their descent from William de Urchard, who fought for Robert the Bruce between 1297 and 1328.

variants.name: Orcutt
meanings: English (Staffordshire): perhaps a much altered form of Scottish   Urquhart  .

variants.name: Orchard
meanings: topographic or occupational name from residence near (or perhaps employment at) an orchard, Middle English orchard (Old English ortgeard, orceard).

habitational name from any of the places called Orchard. Those in Devon and Somerset are named from Old English ortgeard, orceard (a compound of wort, wyrt ‘plant’ (later associated with Latin hortus ‘garden’) + geard ‘yard, enclosure’), while East and West Orchard near Shaftesbury in Dorset derive from Primitive Welsh ar ‘beside, in front of, facing’ + ced ‘wood’.
rare variant of Scottish   Urquhart  . Compare   Orahood  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Matilda Urquhart of Troup, Baroness of Dunottar was born in 1360, in Banffshire, Scotland, her father, William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty, was 35 and her mother, Agnes Ramsey, was 35. She married Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland in 1379, in St. Andrews, Orkney, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 2 June 1413, in Kincardineshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Matilda Urquhart of Troup, Baroness of Dunottar est née en 1360, à Banffshire, Écosse, son père, William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Agnes Ramsey, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland en 1379, à St. Andrews, Orkney, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 2 juin 1413, à Kincardineshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans.

Sie hat 1379 in St. Andrews, Orkney, Schottland, United Kingdom Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 2. ko.lifeSummary: Lady Matilda Urquhart of Troup, Baroness of Dunottar(이)가 1360년 스컷랜드 반프실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Ramsey(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1379년 영국 스코틀랜드 르니 스트. 앤드루스에서 Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1413년 6월 2일 영국 스코틀랜드 킨카딘실르에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Matilda Urquhart of Troup, Baroness of Dunottar родилась в 1360 г. в местности банффшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Ramsey, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland в 1379 г. в местности ст. андрюс, орни, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 2 июня 1413 г. в местности кинкардаинешир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет.

Se casó con Sir Robert de Keith Great Marischal of Scotland en 1379, en St. Andrews, Orkney, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 2 de junio de 1413, en Kincardineshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 53 años.
